title: Older Adult/Disability Related Supportive Housing - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Diverse Populations > Older Adult/Disability Related Supportive Housing 4 Matching Service Providers Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF ALBERTINA KERR ADULT GROUP HOMES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides homes for adults with I/DD, including autism, supporting each person’s independence, skill-building, and community participation. Residents have access to behavioral and mental health services, medication administration, self-advocacy training, and connections to community activities, including education, vocational training, or employment. Staff help with daily self-care while encouraging independent living skills like shopping and managing money. Residents have private bedrooms and share common areas with housemates who have similar interests and support needs. The program does not provide 2:1 or 1:1 caregiver-to-resident staffing ratios. 424 NE 22nd Avenue Portland, OR 97232 Eligibility: Age 18 and older with an I/DD diagnosis, including autism Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 239-8101 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MOUNTAIN VIEW COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT SAFE PARKING Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides safe parking facilities for community members living out of their car or a RV, and also has shower and laundry facilities. Partners with two local low-income housing complexes to provide case management support for placement into permanent housing. 1475 SW 35th Street Redmond, OR 97756 Eligibility: Individuals experiencing homelessness Permanent housing: age 55 and older or families with children Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4pm Email (541) 527-0028 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON SUPPORTED LIVING PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides 24-hour residential settings and supported living services for people who live independently. Staff help create supportive home environments that foster individual growth and independence while maximizing health and safety. Offers assistance with banking, budgeting, medical care, bill paying, community inclusion, etc. Residential homes are located throughout the Eugene-Springfield area. 412 Pearl Street Eugene, OR 97401 Eligibility: Age 18 and older with developmental disabilities that are eligible for public funding or have sufficient resources for private pay Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm (541) 343-4196 Get Directions Visit Website More Details WASHINGTON COUNTY DISABILITY, AGING AND VETERAN SERVICES CARE FACILITIES AND SENIOR HOUSING Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides resources to help individuals and families find adult foster homes, residential care facilities, assisted living facilities, and nursing facilities. Lists contain contact information and whether the facility accepts Medicaid clients. Long-term care facility search website provides additional details about each facility. 5240 NE Elam Young Parkway Suite 300 Hillsboro, OR 97124 Eligibility: Varies by service Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 846-3060 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ALBERTINA KERR ADULT GROUP HOMES MOUNTAIN VIEW COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT SAFE PARKING OREGON SUPPORTED LIVING PROGRAM OREGON SUPPORTED LIVING PROGRAM WASHINGTON COUNTY DISABILITY, AGING AND VETERAN SERVICES CARE FACILITIES AND SENIOR HOUSING
